outputs.description: If true, ensures that all TMS appliances or Cisco ASR 9000 vDDoS Protection devices in a group use less than 90% of the allowed traffic rate in order for a mitigation to start. NASightline.TMSGroup.check_bgp_peering If true, ensures that the TMS appliances or Cisco ASR 9000 vDDoS Protection devices are part of a peering session.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.check_available_bw

outputs.description: If true, requires all group members to be up before starting a mitigation. This ensures that sufficient resources are available before a mitigation starts. NASightline.TMSGroup.default_bgp_offramp If true, the default BGP diversion nexthops of the TMS appliances or Cisco ASR 9000 vDDoS Protection devices in the TMS group are used.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.check_group_allup

outputs.description: Description of the TMS group. NASightline.TMSGroup.fail_open If true, ends the mitigation if one or more group members fails or becomes unreachable.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.description

outputs.description: In TMS flowspec diversion deployments, these attributes each specify a destination route target or IP address. The Sightline peer uses these destinations in TMS mitigations to advertise routes to its BGP peers. NASightline.TMSGroup.flowspec_redirect_ipv4_type In TMS flowspec diversion deployments, these attributes define whether the Sightline peer redirects TMS mitigation traffic to a route target or to an IP address.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.flowspec_redirect_ipv4_destination

outputs.description: In TMS flowspec diversion deployments, these attributes each specify a destination route target or IP address. The Sightline peer uses these destinations in TMS mitigations to advertise routes to its BGP peers. NASightline.TMSGroup.flowspec_redirect_ipv6_type In TMS flowspec diversion deployments, these attributes define whether the Sightline peer redirects TMS mitigation traffic to a route target or to an IP address.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.flowspec_redirect_ipv6_destination

outputs.description: The TMS group ID. NASightline.TMSGroup.l3vpn_flowspec_ipv4_route_distinguisher The route distinguisher (RD) for a VPN, which uniquely identifies the routes for that VPN.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.id

outputs.description: The route distinguisher (RD) for a VPN, which uniquely identifies the routes for that VPN. NASightline.TMSGroup.member_limits_differ If true, TMS device limits (such as maximum mitigations or filter lists) differ, which leads to either performance issues if devices change midstream for ongoing mitigations, or failure to start or save mitigations.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.l3vpn_flowspec_ipv6_route_distinguisher

outputs.description: The percentage of total bandwidth capacity at which this TMS group will become overloaded. NASightline.TMSGroup.mitigation_orchestration.enabled If true, mitigation orchestration is enabled for this TMS group.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.mitigation_orchestration.bandwidth_threshold_percent

outputs.description: TMS group name. NASightline.TMSGroup.nexthop The IPv4 address for the BGP diversion nexthop. It overrides the default nexthops of the TMS appliances or Cisco ASR 9000 vDDoS Protection devices that are in the TMS group.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.name

outputs.description: The IPv6 address for the BGP diversion nexthop. It overrides the default nexthops of the TMS appliances or Cisco ASR 9000 vDDoS Protection devices that are in the TMS group. NASightline.TMSGroup.relationships Relationships of the managed object (only visible when extending the data).
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.nexthop_v6

outputs.description: If true, the TMS group is pre-configured in Sightline and is not editable. NASightline.TMSGroup.tms_group_type Type of the TMS group.
outputs.name: NASightline.TMSGroup.system
